content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

message.content: The app displays "try again in 6 hours" when profile metric fetching is triggered, even though the Apify api key isn't rate limited. The rate limiting in our own app is limiting me from demo-ing the app. Decrease the rate limits, since this app won't be released for production anyways

addedBlocks:
## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- feature-dev:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus

- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead

- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically ques…

- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

scripts/ralph/progress.txt:501:  - Story AC says "body `{platform}`, throttle on `metric_snapshots.created_at`"; spec §5.2 says "body `{social_link_id}`, throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`". Followed the spec per the Ralph "specs win" rule. Response also extends with `metrics_status` to match US-020's shape so the client can share rendering code.
scripts/ralph/progress.txt:504:  - Per-run `last_scrape_attempt_at` bump is a budget protector, not just a throttle primitive. Bumping BEFORE dispatch means a dispatch that panics or times out still costs the caller 6h — worth it to stop a flaky network from becoming an expense amplifier. Document this tradeoff explicitly in the edge-function header whenever you pick the "bump early" side.

supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql:69:  last_scrape_error       text,

content.content:
1	// Edge function: auth-signup-creator (US-019 + US-020)
2	//
3	// Contract:
4	//   POST { username: string, tiktok_handle?: string, instagram_handle?: string }
5	//     → 200 {
6	//         token: string,
7	//         user_id: string,
8	//         role: 'creator',
9	//         metrics_status: { tiktok?: Status, ig_details?: Status, ig_posts?: Status },
10	//       }
11	//       where Status is 'fresh' | 'refreshing' | 'failed'.
12	//     → 400 { error: 'INVALID_REQUEST' | 'INVALID_JSON' }
13	//     → 422 { error: 'HANDLE_REQUIRED' }
14	//     → 409 { error: 'USERNAME_TAKEN' | 'HANDLE_TAKEN' }
15	//     → 500 { error: 'SERVER_MISCONFIGURED' | 'DB_ERROR' | 'JWT_ERROR' }
16	//
17	// Auth: none. This is how a creator obtains their first JWT, so the
18	// endpoint is verify_jwt=false at the gateway. The handler itself mints
19	// the token via _shared/jwt.ts (HS256, MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET).
20	//
21	// Atomicity: the users + creator_profiles + social_links inserts run
22	// inside a single Postgres transaction via the public.auth_signup_creator
23	// RPC (us_019 migration). A failure on any insert rolls back the flow.
24	// Apify dispatch happens AFTER the RPC commits — per spec §5.1, a
25	// dispatch failure does NOT roll back the account (the user gets their
26	// JWT, metrics_status carries the per-platform outcome so the client can
27	// decide whether to retry later or surface a 'refreshing' placeholder).
28	//
29	// Handle normalization: leading '@' is stripped and whitespace trimmed
30	// before the RPC call. Handles are compared case-insensitively (citext),
31	// matching social_links.handle's column type.
32	//
33	// Apify dispatch (US-020): once the RPC commits, we look up the
34	// freshly-inserted social_links rows, then kick one or more Apify runs in
35	// parallel via Promise.allSettled — tiktok_profile for TikTok handles;
36	// ig_details and ig_posts for Instagram handles. Each run is registered
37	// with a per-run webhook pointing back at /functions/v1/apify-webhook so
38	// the receiver can persist the metric_snapshots row when Apify finishes.
39	// The wait window is APIFY_WAIT_SECS (60s); runs that come back
40	// SUCCEEDED within the window are reported as 'fresh' (the webhook will
41	// also arrive and be deduped via the unique index on run_id), otherwise
42	// 'refreshing' and the client polls. A thrown dispatch is 'failed'.
43	//
44	// Pre-refreshing rows: the spec calls for pre-creating metric_snapshots
45	// rows in status='refreshing' before the scrape so the client has
46	// something to read. That's deferred — the existing US-016..US-018 RPCs
47	// INSERT on webhook arrival via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, so the row
48	// appears only once Apify finishes. Tracked as a spec gap in progress.txt.
49	//
50	// Rate limit: not implemented yet. Spec §5.1 requires per-IP throttling

lastPrompt: The app displays "try again in 6 hours" when profile metric fetching is triggered, even though the Apify api key isn't rate limited. The rate limiting in our own app is limiting me from demo-ing the a…

content.text: Found it. The throttle is in `supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts:95` — `THROTTLE_SECS = 6 * 60 * 60` (6h). I'll drop it to 30 seconds for demo purposes.

content.content: The file /Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +// Demo build: throttle reduced from 6h to 30s so the app can be exercised
+// without waiting out the production cooldown. Revert to `6 * 60 * 60`

content.text:
Changed `THROTTLE_SECS` from `6 * 60 * 60` (6h) to `30` seconds in `supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts:95`. This is the only throttle point — the client just renders `retry_after_sec` from the server's 429.

Redeploy the edge function for it to take effect:

```
supabase functions deploy metrics-refresh
```

diff --git a/scripts/ralph/prd.json b/scripts/ralph/prd.json
index 2b5b259..498fa5f 100644
--- a/scripts/ralph/prd.json
+++ b/scripts/ralph/prd.json
@@ -661,7 +661,7 @@
       ],
       "priority": 44,
       "passes": false,
-      "notes": "2026-04-18: Implementation complete + code-reviewed, but E2E realtime delivery verification BLOCKED by Supabase Realtime tenant infrastructure failure for project [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]. Server-side realtime logs show persistent 'UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database' every 5-15s (even after the probe stops), with one prior 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 494396 ms' (~8min) suggesting a stuck tenant cold-start path \u2014 this is beyond what the client can recover from. Work shipped this iteration: (1) migration us_044_applications_realtime_publication adds public.applications to supabase_realtime (default replica identity is sufficient because the client only reads payload.new.id+status); (2) app/(creator)/applications.tsx extended with a useEffect that calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()) then subscribes to UPDATE events on applications filtered by creator_id=eq.<me>, patches rows in state on a status change, and fires the AC-specified success/error toast ('Your application was approved/rejected') for approved and rejected only (other 4 enum values patch the row silently since AC only names the two decisions); (3) a useRef mirror of `rows` (Codebase Pattern #82/#106) prevents stale-closure reads inside the subscription callback without forcing re-subscription on every list mutation; (4) mobile-mcp captured us-044-applied-pending-empty.png + us-044-applied-approved-empty.png proving the subscription wiring doesn't crash the screen under the DevRolePicker creator session (subscription is no-op there because the dev token is a literal string, not a signed JWT \u2014 same posture as US-036/US-043 per Codebase Pattern #122); (5) scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts two-mode probe (signup | subscribe) that mints a real Marketify JWT via auth-signup-creator, calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(jwt), subscribes to UPDATE events under the same RLS policy (applications_creator_rw, polcmd='*') that guards the in-app stream, and asserts payload.new.status matches expected \u2014 ran end-to-end this iteration and returned FAIL with '[channel] status=TIMED_OUT' repeated four times because the broker never reached SUBSCRIBED. Probe test fixture (user_id a3495acd-7241-4d41-8e15-d42a210fdf65 + cascading rows) was cleaned up immediately via execute_sql. Code-reviewer returned 2 IMPORTANT findings, both carried over unchanged from US-036 (stale-token-at-subscription-time baseline across all realtime screens) and both AC-conformant (toast fires only on approved/rejected, matching the exact AC wording). Next iteration should re-run the probe (bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup then subscribe) once Supabase Realtime tenant is healthy \u2014 if verdict PASS, flip passes:true. Tenant hibernation/cold-start is the likely root cause; waiting for the tenant to warm up + checking get_logs({service:'realtime'}) for 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (the postgres_changes slot, not just the messages slot) before running the probe is the recommended order. | 2026-04-18 06:43 re-verify: realtime tenant still broken \u2014 same TIMED_OUT pattern. New probe run signed up user 7ca930b8-...2297, seeded application 1d5d3800-...6350 against listing 11111111-...010, subscribed \u2192 4x TIMED_OUT then verdict FAIL. get_logs({service:realtime}) confirms only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 ever starts; the postgres_changes slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2 has not appeared in 24h. CheckConnection latency oscillates 476ms (healthy) \u2194 670s+ (cold-start) \u2194 tenant-shutdown-on-idle. No code change this iteration; fixtures cleaned up. Next iteration: re-run the probe as before; if get_logs shows the postgres_changes slot starting (search for slot name without the messages_ infix), the broker is finally healthy and the probe should reach SUBSCRIBED. | 2026-04-18 09:58 re-verify #3: tenant still broken. Per Codebase Pattern #124 decision rule, did NOT run the probe this iteration because get_logs({service:'realtime'}) STILL shows only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 starting (never supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2, the postgres_changes slot). Recent realtime log entries (last 24h window): 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 672448 ms' + 'executed in 800322 ms' + 'executed in 672573 ms' (3 cold-starts each >11min), 'UnableToConnectToProject' flaring every 5-15s, tenant repeatedly hibernating ('Stop tenant [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF] because of no connected users') \u2192 re-initializing \u2192 timing out again. The single 'executed in 476 ms' in the window was immediately followed by another hibernation cycle. Skipping probe execution saves a fresh probe-user fixture + cleanup cycle that would predictably TIMED_OUT; the get_logs observation is authoritative per the established pattern. Code unchanged. Next iteration: re-check get_logs first; only run the probe once you see 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (WITHOUT the _messages_ infix) followed by absence of 'UnableToConnectToProject' for at least 60s. Supabase-side dashboard action (tenant restart / support ticket) may be required if the pattern persists into iteration #4+."

Observed: 5 fresh 'UnableToConnectToProject' entries, CheckConnection latencies of 561ms (one healthy) vs 670537ms/672448ms/708088ms (three ~11min cold-starts), and repeated 'Stop tenant [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF] because of no connected users' hibernations re-triggering the failed initialization cycle. No code change this iteration; work moves to US-056 which also requires realtime (same posture: ship implementation, defer end-to-end realtime verification to when tenant is healthy). Next iteration: re-check get_logs; only run probe-us044-realtime.ts once 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (WITHOUT _messages_ infix) appears and 'UnableToConnectToProject' is absent for 60s+. Dashboard-side tenant restart / support ticket almost certainly needed by this point."

Work shipped this iteration: (1) migration us_044_applications_realtime_publication adds public.applications to supabase_realtime (default replica identity is sufficient because the client only reads payload.new.id+status); (2) app/(creator)/applications.tsx extended with a useEffect that calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()) then subscribes to UPDATE events on applications filtered by creator_id=eq.<me>, patches rows in state on a status change, and fires the AC-specified success/error toast ('Your application was approved/rejected') for approved and rejected only (other 4 enum values patch the row silently since AC only names the two decisions); (3) a useRef mirror of `rows` (Codebase Pattern #82/#106) prevents stale-closure reads inside the subscription callback without forcing re-subscription on every list mutation; (4) mobile-mcp captured us-044-applied-pending-empty.png + us-044-applied-approved-empty.png proving the subscription wiring doesn't crash the screen under the DevRolePicker creator session (subscription is no-op there because the dev token is a literal string, not a signed JWT \u2014 same posture as US-036/US-043 per Codebase Pattern #122); (5) scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts two-mode probe (signup | subscribe) that mints a real Marketify JWT via auth-signup-creator, calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(jwt), subscribes to UPDATE events under the same RLS policy (applications_creator_rw, polcmd='*') that guards the in-app stream, and asserts payload.new.status matches expected \u2014 ran end-to-end this iteration and returned FAIL with '[channel] status=TIMED_OUT' repeated four times because the broker never reached SUBSCRIBED. Probe test fixture (user_id a3495acd-7241-4d41-8e15-d42a210fdf65 + cascading rows) was cleaned up immediately via execute_sql. Code-reviewer returned 2 IMPORTANT findings, both carried over unchanged from US-036 (stale-token-at-subscription-time baseline across all realtime screens) and both AC-conformant (toast fires only on approved/rejected, matching the exact AC wording). Next iteration should re-run the probe (bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup then subscribe) once Supabase Realtime tenant is healthy \u2014 if verdict PASS, flip passes:true. Tenant hibernation/cold-start is the likely root cause; waiting for the tenant to warm up + checking get_logs({service:'realtime'}) for 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (the postgres_changes slot, not just the messages slot) before running the probe is the recommended order. | 2026-04-18 06:43 re-verify: realtime tenant still broken \u2014 same TIMED_OUT pattern. New probe run signed up user 7ca930b8-...2297, seeded application 1d5d3800-...6350 against listing 11111111-...010, subscribed \u2192 4x TIMED_OUT then verdict FAIL. get_logs({service:realtime}) confirms only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 ever starts; the postgres_changes slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2 has not appeared in 24h. CheckConnection latency oscillates 476ms (healthy) \u2194 670s+ (cold-start) \u2194 tenant-shutdown-on-idle. No code change this iteration; fixtures cleaned up. Next iteration: re-run the probe as before; if get_logs shows the postgres_changes slot starting (search for slot name without the messages_ infix), the broker is finally healthy and the probe should reach SUBSCRIBED. | 2026-04-18 09:58 re-verify #3: tenant still broken. Per Codebase Pattern #124 decision rule, did NOT run the probe this iteration because get_logs({service:'realtime'}) STILL shows only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 starting (never supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2, the postgres_changes slot). Recent realtime log entries (last 24h window): 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 672448 ms' + 'executed in 800322 ms' + 'executed in 672573 ms' (3 cold-starts each >11min), 'UnableToConnectToProject' flaring every 5-15s, tenant repeatedly hibernating ('Stop tenant [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF] because of no connected users') \u2192 re-initializing \u2192 timing out again. The single 'executed in 476 ms' in the window was immediately followed by another hibernation cycle. Skipping probe execution saves a fresh probe-user fixture + cleanup cycle that would predictably TIMED_OUT; the get_logs observation is authoritative per the established pattern. Code unchanged. Next iteration: re-check get_logs first; only run the probe once you see 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (WITHOUT the _messages_ infix) followed by absence of 'UnableToConnectToProject' for at least 60s. Supabase-side dashboard action (tenant restart / support ticket) may be required if the pattern persists into iteration #4+. | 2026-04-18 13:05 re-verify #4: tenant still broken. Followed the established decision rule (Codebase Pattern #124) and did NOT run the probe this iteration. get_logs({service:'realtime'}) over the last ~24h still shows only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 starting (never the postgres_changes slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2). Observed: 5 fresh 'UnableToConnectToProject' entries, CheckConnection latencies of 561ms (one healthy) vs 670537ms/672448ms/708088ms (three ~11min cold-starts), and repeated 'Stop tenant [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF] because of no connected users' hibernations re-triggering the failed initialization cycle. No code change this iteration; work moves to US-056 which also requires realtime (same posture: ship implementation, defer end-to-end realtime verification to when tenant is healthy). Next iteration: re-check get_logs; only run probe-us044-realtime.ts once 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (WITHOUT _messages_ infix) appears and 'UnableToConnectToProject' is absent for 60s+. Dashboard-side tenant restart / support ticket almost certainly needed by this point."
     },
     {
       "id": "US-045",

content.text: There's a lot in-flight from the Ralph loop (prd.json notes, new migrations, generated types, deleted inbox.tsx). I'll commit only the throttle change to avoid bundling Ralph's in-progress work.

input.command:
git add supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(metrics-refresh): drop throttle from 6h to 30s for demo

Demo builds were hitting the 6h per-link cooldown and surfacing
"try again in 6h" toasts. Lowered THROTTLE_SECS so the app can be
exercised end-to-end; comment flags the revert before shipping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
